characters.name: Benny Baroni
characters.description: Benny Baroniis a friend ofTimwho hangs out at the hardware store, and is portrayed byJimmy Labriola. Benny is a bit of a mooch. He is unemployed, mainly because he doesn't like to work. He makes money by gambling and betting on horse races. He does everything he can to get food for free, like coming to soup kitchens for the homeless, inviting himself to parties without bringing gifts or food but mainly by coming to the hardware store for the free coffee and donuts and never buyinganything. He notoriously known as a freeloader - his trademark is drinking coffee and eating donuts. However, there was one episode where Benny was seen actually doing some work, being part of the cast of Tool Time when Al and Tim show off their dream house and display a walk-in freezer. Benny is inside the freezer, playing the role of a butcher.

characters.name: Al Borland
characters.description: Albert "Al" Borlandis portrayed asTim Taylor's long-suffering sidekick and assistant on the show-within-a-showTool Time, as well as one of Tim's closest friends. He is played byRichard Karn. Al Borlandwas born in Seattle, Washington toAlma Borlandand an unnamed father. When Al was little, he would go to the park and wish his dad would play ball with him like he seen other kids do with their dads.

characters.name: Karen Kelly
characters.description: Karen Kellyis the best friend ofJill Taylor. Whenever she came to visit she was always giving her feminist opinions and making fun of Tim which aggravated him. Her nature seemed better suited to Al, who took a shine to Karen. However, upon discovering she has a boyfriend, Al takes the bad news in stride and instead focuses on finding a new woman. She later moves to California to be with her new boyfriend.

characters.name: Heidi Keppert
characters.description: Heidi Keppertis the second "Tool Girl" onTool Timebeginning in Season 3, afterLisaleft the show to go to college. She is portrayed byDebbe Dunning. Initially, the character was seen only on and around theTool Timeset. In later seasons, her personal life crossed paths withTim TaylorandAl Borlandon a number of occasions. She has a daughter namedAmy Keppert, born in Season 6, and a husband namedScott Keppert. By the end of the final season, she was expecting her second child.

characters.name: Lauren
characters.description: LaurenisRandy'sthird girlfriend, but of all of them, his most serious. Born roughly the same year as Randy, she also has a place in the school newspaper. Lauren loved to hear stories about howBradand Randy used to tormentMark. At the beginning of her eleventh grade, she and Randy left on a one-year environmental study to Costa Rica, to study and save endangered species. As of the end of Season 8, her relationship status with Randy is still active.

characters.name: Lillian Patterson
characters.description: Lillian PattersonisJill's mother, andTim Taylor's mother-in-law. She is played by actress Polly Holliday.  She is married to COLFred Patterson, USA (Ret). As the wife of an army man, she often had to be strong and hard with her daughters, which creates distance between her and Jill, as she feels as though she can't talk to her mother. Her first appearance is in Season 2's "Much Ado About Nana".  In this episode, it is implied that she was previously quite overweight before the visit and she has been dieting to lose several pounds. During her visits, she usually takes a firm handle with Jill whenever she feels as though she's out of line(such as her bossiness and miss treatment of Tim).

characters.description: Lisais Binford's first "Tool Girl", portrayed byPamela Anderson. As a secondary cast member onTool Time, Lisa's job was to introduceTimandAl, hand them any tool they requested, and sometimes bring in the fan mail or their special guest.

characters.name: Lucille Taylor
characters.description: Lucille Taylor, played by Bonnie Bartlett Lucille Tayloris a recurring character onHome Improvement. She is the widow ofMichael Taylorand the mother ofTim Taylor.  She is played byBonnie Bartlett. Lucille raised Tim and all his brothers after Michael Taylor passed away; Tim was 11 years old.  Raising the boys gave Lucille no time to re-marry or pursue another relationship, although she does begin a relationship with Art Leonard, Tim's high school shop teacher.

characters.name: Michael Taylor
characters.description: Michael Tayloris a character onHome Improvement. He is the late father ofTim Taylorand the husband ofLucille Taylor. He is shown in a flashback when Tim helps Lucille move out of the house he grew up in.

characters.name: Morgan Wandell
characters.description: Morgan Wandell(played by Danny Zorn) is a character featured in the final episodes ofHome Improvement. Morgan is underhanded and somewhat cocky, serene in his own experience as a producer.  He prefers to listen to himself and does not accept suggestions from anyone else underneath him.  Morgan is introduced byBud Harperin the episodeDead Weightas the new vice-president of media production for Binford Tools.  Bud reveals to Tim that he himself chose Morgan to superviseTool Time, but Tim immediately takes a dislike to Morgan because of all the changes that are already being made to the show, not the least of which being that the show is now calledBinford's Tool Time, and that the Binford logo is plastered everywhere on the set (including the rear end of Heidi's shorts). Morgan boasts of his experience in entertainment production, particularly reality television, and Bud feels that Morgan has some good ideas forTool Time, including what Morgan calls "explosion consistency"; in other words, Morgan wants to see more stuff blown up on the show "whether it's an accident...  or not", as he puts it.  But Tim refuses to stage accidents on the show, preferring to cite his own stupidity rather than run the risk of someone else getting hurt.

characters.name: Pete Bilker
characters.description: Peter Bilkeris a recurring character onHome Improvement, portrayed byMickey Jones. As a member of the construction crew onTool Time, Peter is best known for his deadpan delivery, imposing presence, and his signature catchphrase:“I don’t think so, Tim.”Though he rarely takes center stage, Peter’s appearances add humor, personality, and a sense of camaraderie to the show’s behind‑the‑scenes world. His character embodies the blue‑collar charm and comedic timing that helped define the series’ workplace segments.
